oneLineSummary: Conditional statements let programs make decisions by choosing different paths based on boolean conditions.
summary: We covered how if/else statements allow programs to branch based on conditions: evaluation, selection, chaining, logical operators, and common pitfalls like = vs ==.
keyTakeaways: Conditions evaluate to True/False and determine which branch runs
Only one branch in if/elif/else executes
Use logical operators (and/or/not) to combine conditions
memoryTricks: Think of a fork in the road — the condition determines your path
preInterviewChecklist: Can you write a leap year checker from memory?
